description: F5 BIG-IP edge-appliance CVEs — TMUI RCE (CVE-2020-5902) and iControl REST auth bypass (CVE-2022-1388). Detection-only endpoint fingerprints.

tmui_rce_2020.description: CVE-2020-5902 — F5 BIG-IP TMUI (Traffic Management UI) auth bypass → RCE via a path-traversal semicolon trick. Reaching the tmshCmd workspace unauthenticated confirms exposure; the benign probe issues no command.

probes.description: 200 reaching tmshCmd.jsp through the /..;/ traversal = TMUI auth bypass present (CVE-2020-5902). No fileName/command supplied — detection only.
F5 BIG-IP TMUI login fingerprint (BIG-IP branding / BIGIPAuthCookie) — confirms the affected management UI is exposed.

icontrol_rest_authbypass_2022.description: CVE-2022-1388 — F5 BIG-IP iControl REST authentication bypass via X-F5-Auth-Token / Connection header smuggling to the mgmt endpoint. Detection = confirm the iControl REST endpoint is reachable; the auth-bypass payload is operator-supervised.

probes.description: iControl REST authn endpoint present (401/200 with F5 JSON error/token) = the CVE-2022-1388 attack surface is reachable. Fingerprint only.
